cases.question: How many news articles in our database are positive, neutral, or negative in tone? Give me a breakdown by sentiment category.

CYPHER 25

WITH org, count(a) AS coverage
ORDER BY coverage DESC

  WHERE a.sentiment IS NOT NULL

  ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC

}
RETURN org.name AS company, a.title AS top_article, a.sentiment AS sentiment
ORDER BY sentiment DESC

cases.question: Which 5 companies have the most total connections in our database — counting ownership relationships, news mentions, and industry category links combined?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Organization)
WITH c,
  COUNT { (c)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() } +
  COUNT { ()-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(c) } AS ownershipLinks,
  COUNT { ()-[:MENTIONS]->(c) }       AS mentionLinks,
  COUNT { (c)-[:IN_CATEGORY]-() }     AS categoryLinks
WITH c,
  ownershipLinks + mentionLinks + categoryLinks AS totalConnections
ORDER BY totalConnections DESC
LIMIT 5
RETURN c.name AS company, totalConnections

cases.question: For the 20 most-covered companies in our news database, show how many subsidiaries each owns and what the average tone of their press coverage is. Rank by most negative coverage first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (org)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
WITH org, count(DISTINCT a) AS articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC
LIMIT 20
OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH org, articleCount, count(sub) AS subsidiaryCount
MATCH (a2:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org)
WITH org, articleCount, subsidiaryCount, avg(a2.sentiment) AS avgSentiment
ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC
RETURN org.name AS company,
       articleCount,
       subsidiaryCount,
       round(avgSentiment, 4) AS avgSentiment

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which company pairs appear in both positive and negative news stories? I'm looking for companies that generate mixed sentiment coverage together. Show the top 10 pairs.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(c1:Organization),
      (a)-[:MENTIONS]->(c2:Organization)
WHERE c1.name < c2.name
  AND NOT EXISTS { (c1)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
  AND NOT EXISTS { (c2)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
WITH c1.name AS company1, c2.name AS company2,
     sum(CASE WHEN a.sentiment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS positiveArticles,
     sum(CASE WHEN a.sentiment < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS negativeArticles,
     count(a) AS totalArticles
WHERE positiveArticles > 0 AND negativeArticles > 0
RETURN company1, company2, positiveArticles, negativeArticles, totalArticles,
       round(toFloat(positiveArticles - negativeArticles) / totalArticles * 100, 1) AS sentimentBalance
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

WHERE NOT EXISTS { (c)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }

cases.question: Which pairs of companies in the same industry category are also covered together in the same news articles? Show the category, the company pair, and how often they appear together.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (cat:Category)<-[:IN_CATEGORY]-(c1:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(c2:Organization)-[:IN_CATEGORY]->(cat)
WHERE c1 <> c2
  AND NOT EXISTS { (c1)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
  AND NOT EXISTS { (c2)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
  AND elementId(c1) < elementId(c2)
RETURN cat.name AS category,
       c1.name AS company1,
       c2.name AS company2,
       count(DISTINCT a) AS co_appearances
ORDER BY co_appearances DESC
LIMIT 50

RETURN c.name AS company, subsidiaries, categories

cases.question: By industry category, give me a comprehensive picture: how many companies are in each category, how many news articles mention them in total, what is the average sentiment of that coverage, and how many subsidiaries exist across the group? Show the top 10 categories by total article count.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (cat:Category)<-[:IN_CATEGORY]-(c:Company)
WITH cat, collect(c) AS companies
WITH cat,
     size(companies) AS companyCount,
     [co IN companies | elementId(co)] AS companyIds
UNWIND companies AS c
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-(sub:Company)
WITH cat, companyCount,
     count(DISTINCT a) AS totalArticles,
     avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment,
     count(DISTINCT sub) AS totalSubsidiaries
RETURN cat.name AS category,
       companyCount,
       totalArticles,
       round(avgSentiment, 3) AS avgSentiment,
       totalSubsidiaries
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

cases.question: Search for Microsoft in our company database. For each match, show its industry category, the top 3 news headlines about it, how many news articles mention it, and how many subsidiaries it has.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('entity', $searchTerm) YIELD node AS org, score
WHERE org:Organization
WITH org, score
ORDER BY score DESC
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:IN_CATEGORY]->(cat:IndustryCategory)
  RETURN collect(DISTINCT cat.name) AS industries
}
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
  RETURN count(DISTINCT a) AS articleCount
}
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)-[:HAS_CHUNK]->(c:Chunk)
  WITH a
  ORDER BY a.date DESC
  RETURN collect(DISTINCT a.title)[..3] AS topHeadlines
}
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
  RETURN count(DISTINCT sub) AS subsidiaryCount
}
RETURN org.name AS company,
       industries,
       topHeadlines,
       articleCount,
       subsidiaryCount
ORDER BY score DESC

gate_details.reason: Execution error: {neo4j_code: Neo.ClientError.Statement.ParameterMissing} {message: Expected parameter(s): searchTerm} {gql_status: 42001} {gql_status_description: error: syntax error or access rule violation - invalid syntax}

cases.question: For companies with at least 5 subsidiaries, show the most positively-covered news article about each (if any), and list the names of companies that sit two levels down in their ownership hierarchy. Return up to 10 companies.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (parent:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH parent, count(sub) AS subCount
WHERE subCount >= 5
WITH parent ORDER BY subCount DESC LIMIT 10

OPTIONAL MATCH (parent)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
WITH parent, a ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC
WITH parent, collect(a)[0] AS topArticle

OPTIONAL MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(level2:Organization)
WITH parent, topArticle, collect(DISTINCT level2.name) AS secondLevelCompanies

RETURN
  parent.name AS company,
  topArticle.title AS topArticleTitle,
  topArticle.sentiment AS topArticleSentiment,
  secondLevelCompanies
ORDER BY size(secondLevelCompanies) DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Classify every company into one of four groups: those with both subsidiaries and press coverage, those with subsidiaries but no press coverage, those with press coverage but no subsidiaries, and those with neither. How many companies fall into each group?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (c)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
WITH c,
  EXISTS { (c)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() } AS hasSubs,
  EXISTS { ()-[:MENTIONS]->(c) } AS hasPress
WITH
  CASE
    WHEN hasSubs AND hasPress THEN 'Both subsidiaries and press coverage'
    WHEN hasSubs AND NOT hasPress THEN 'Subsidiaries only'
    WHEN NOT hasSubs AND hasPress THEN 'Press coverage only'
    ELSE 'Neither'
  END AS group
RETURN group, count(*) AS companyCount
ORDER BY companyCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
